title: paepalanthus blepharophorus
intro: Paepalanthus blepharophorus, also known as the 'eyelash Paepalanthus' due to its distinctive leaf shape resembling human eyelashes, is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. Traditionally, Paepalanthus blepharophorus has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. The plant is often prepared for herbal use by drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or capsules.
